text: Recreational runners complete a half-marathon at approximately 79% of VO2max on average.
VO2max correlates well with half-marathon performance time in recreational runners.
Running speed at 4 mmol/L blood lactate concentration is a stronger predictor of half-marathon performance than VO2max alone.
Post-race blood lactate concentration after a half-marathon averages approximately 5.65 mmol/L in recreational runners, indicating the race is run above lactate threshold.
Energy expenditure per kilogram per minute correlates very strongly with half-marathon performance time, making it a better predictor than total energy expenditure.
Estimated total energy expenditure for a half-marathon in recreational runners is approximately 6.22 MJ (~1,487 kcal).
